title: Liquid from an Object
result_description: Coffee appears to pour out of a phone screen into the cup below: you set the phone on top of the glass, push down, and the liquid fills the cup as if the phone produced it.

inputs.name: pour_clip
inputs.what_to_film: On a locked-off, exposure-locked camera, pour coffee into the clear glass; keep arms and shadows clear of the glass so the area is clean for masking.

capture_requirements.note: lock exposure on the countertop next to the glass; pour from the lit side to avoid shadows

inputs.name: phone_push_clip
inputs.what_to_film: Same locked-off frame: set the phone on top of the glass and push down with your fingers; do not let your hand pass behind the glass (it is see-through and breaks the illusion).

capture_requirements.note: keep hands in front of the glass, never behind it

params.note: isolate the pour section and the phone-push section; delete the rest
coffee pour goes on the top layer; phone_push is the main/base layer
temporarily lower so you can line up the timing of the two actions
slide so the coffee starts pouring right as the phone is pushed down

params.position: around the glass, just below the phone / on top of the cup
params.note: shape it to the glass so only the liquid inside shows
soften the mask edge along the line below the phone
freeze-frame the duplicate to cover the moment a hand crosses the glass
fade the freeze-frame in/out to hide the hard cut (pop)
keyframe/duck volume to remove pre-pour talking

result.description: Coffee pours out of the phone into the cup with a seamless join.
success_criteria: Liquid level rises in sync with the push-down
No visible mask line below the phone
No pop or hand crossing behind the glass
